title: viola biflora subsp. biflora
intro: Viola biflora subsp. biflora, also known as the Alpine pansy or Fragrant pansy, is a perennial plant species in the violet family (Violaceae) that is native to Europe and Asia. The plant's roots and leaves have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Viola biflora subsp. biflora include its ability to reduce inflammation, treat wounds, and alleviate symptoms of respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Viola biflora subsp. biflora can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, tinctures, and salves, which are often used to treat a range of health conditions.
